systemprompt.text:
Act as a professional Social Media Strategist. Using the inputs below, generate a 30-day content calendar focused on achieving the Main Goal.

First, do a brief internal research & strategy step using your existing knowledge and, if available, live web data for this Topic/Niche and Target Audience. Clarify the audience's main pain points and desires, the types of posts and hooks that perform well in this niche, and any relevant current trends. Use these insights only to guide the calendar, do NOT output them separately.

The output MUST be a Markdown table with these exact columns (and nothing else): Day #, Date, Theme/Pillar, Platform, Content Idea, Call to Action (CTA).

Calendar guidelines:
- Create exactly 30 rows, one for each consecutive calendar day starting from the Start Date, with real dates in ascending order.
- The Theme/Pillar in each row must be one of the provided Content Pillars, with a reasonably balanced mix across the 30 days.
- The Platform column must follow the Posting Schedule. If multiple platforms are specified for a given day, create one row per platform.

Content guidelines:
- Each Content Idea must state the post type (e.g., Reel, carousel, static post, story, live, poll, LinkedIn text post) and include a clear, scroll-stopping hook tailored to the Target Audience.
- Each CTA must be specific, action-oriented, and directly support the Main Goal (e.g., "Download the free guide", "Book a call", "Join the newsletter", "Comment with your #1 question").
- Vary formats and angles across the 30 days (e.g., educational, storytelling, behind-the-scenes, FAQs, objections, social proof, engagement prompts) to avoid repetition.
- Make every idea clearly connected to the Target Audience's key pain points and desires and aligned with the Main Goal, guided by the research step above.

Inputs (fill these in before running the prompt):
* Topic/Niche: [Insert your Topic/Niche here]
* Target Audience: [Insert your Target Audience here, including key pain points & desires if known]
* Main Goal: [Insert your Main Goal here, e.g., grow email list / drive sales / build authority]
* Posting Schedule: [Insert your Posting Frequency & Platform here, e.g., 5x/week on IG Reels + 3x/week on LinkedIn]
* Content Pillars: [Insert your 3–5 Content Pillars here, separated by commas]
* Start Date: [Insert the first date of your calendar, e.g., November 18, 2025]

Only output the final Markdown table. Do NOT include explanations, notes, or any text outside the table.

description.text: AI-powered development tools with REST API, CLI, privacy, and no MCP setup needed

message.text:
# 👨‍💻 Digital team mates for developers

OpenMates is also an awesome tool for developers. For everything from quick questions, planning large projects, generating code based on the most up-to-date docs and much more. All without any complicated MCP server setups and all while protecting your privacy & giving you access to all the best AI models. A REST API for all app skills included, and a powerful CLI and pip & npm packages are also planned, to allow you to interact with your chats from anywhere.
Click on those example chats to see what OpenMates can do for developers:
[[dev_example_chats_group]]

## 🚀 Quick start

Press the Sign up button in the top right to sign up, buy usage credits and start using OpenMates. Or keep reading to learn more about OpenMates for developers, and check the example chats in the chats menu in the top left to explore what OpenMates can do.

## 📱 Apps for developers

With Apps on OpenMates, there is no need for complicated, time-consuming and insecure MCP setups with clear text API keys in JSON config files.
Instead, OpenMates will automatically use the right AI model, app skill, focus mode, settings & memories optimized for your request (while allowing you to overwrite the auto selection via @ in your message).
Some examples of how Apps make OpenMates more useful:
- 📖 [Code | Get docs](#settings/appstore/code/skills/get-docs): Checks the latest documentation for an API, framework or language before generating code files.
- 🔍 [Web | Search](#settings/appstore/web/skills/search): Searches the web to improve response accuracy.
- ✅ Auto linter checks for generated code (coming soon).
[[dev_app_store_group]]

### 🛠️ Developer skills

Skills specific to developer apps that allow your mates to interact with external developer services.
[[dev_skills_group]]

### 🎯 Developer focus modes

Focus modes for developer tasks like planning projects, writing tests, improving security, and more.
[[dev_focus_modes_group]]

### 💾 Developer settings & memories

Settings & memories for developer apps, so you don't need to mention project details, preferred tech stack, or frameworks all the time.
[[dev_settings_memories_group]]

## 🔌 REST API

The OpenMates REST API allows you to use OpenMates similar to other LLM APIs, and interact with all the App skills from OpenMates. So you can implement OpenMates and its App skills easily into your own software.

## 💻 CLI, pip & npm packages - a usability-focused approach

The soon-to-be-released CLI and pip & npm packages will allow you full access to your OpenMates account from anywhere. While ensuring zero-knowledge encryption and you staying in control. Access all your chats and App skills from your terminal.
Or task OpenMates with building or debugging a software project on your VM / dev server, but with the convenience of managing that task in your web browser instead of from a terminal.

## 🔒 Privacy & encryption

OpenMates never trains any models on any of your data. And all your chats, project details and other app settings & memories are zero-knowledge encrypted & decrypted on your device. When you make a request, only the minimum amount of data needed to fulfill your request is processed on the server and never stored on disk in a way that the server or hackers could decrypt the chats or your App settings & memories.

## 🛡️ Open source as enshittification protection

User interests first, is the core philosophy behind OpenMates. To ensure this also in the future, [OpenMates is fully open source under AGPL 3](https://github.com/glowingkitty/openmates). Because being open source is the foundation for preventing enshittification from making the software worse over time.
In addition, you can check the architecture & security of OpenMates at any time yourself and are welcome to contribute, to make the project even better for everyone.
If you want to self-host OpenMates and take care of all the setup and maintenance, you can as well. Using your own API keys for the various AI services, and in the future also using offline models, for maximum privacy and control.

---

📚 Did you already read the introduction for everyone? If not, then [click here](#chat-id=demo-for-everyone) to learn all the core basics of OpenMates.

message.text:
# ✨ Digital team mates for everyone

With OpenMates you have a team of specialized AI chatbots that can not only inspire you & teach new knowledge but also fulfill various tasks using apps. Making the useful aspects of AI accessible to everyone, while protecting your privacy & giving you access to all the best AI models.
Click on those example chats to see what OpenMates can do:
[[example_chats_group]]

## 🚀 Quick start

Press the Sign up button in the top right to sign up, buy usage credits and start using OpenMates. Or keep reading to learn more about OpenMates, and check the example chats in the chats menu in the top left to explore what OpenMates can do.

## 📱 Apps

Since only searching the web is not enough for fulfilling everyday tasks, OpenMates supports various other apps.
[[app_store_group]]
In the [App Store](#settings/appstore) you have an overview over all the various apps your digital team mates can use to fulfill your requests. And their skills, focus modes and settings & memories.

### 🛠️ Skills

Allow your mates to interact with external services. To search the web, read video transcripts & websites, search for locations and their opening hours, search for videos and much more.
[[skills_group]]

### 🎯 Focus modes

Change the focus of a chat temporarily to reach a specific goal. Makes a huge difference in getting much better responses & help by your chatbots. Without you having to be an expert in knowing how to design complex requests for chatbots.
[[focus_modes_group]]

### 💾 Settings & memories

Allows OpenMates to give you more personalized answers based on your interests, trips, books you wanted to read, projects and more. But:
- ✅ only when it's needed
- ✅ only for the specific chat where it's needed
- ✅ only with the data you clearly consent to share
- ✅ no sensitive data are ever stored on the server on disk
All settings & memories are encrypted & decrypted on your devices. Just like your chats. This means the server can never decrypt them by itself. Only your devices can, using your login method.
[[settings_memories_group]]

## 🎨 Designed for ease of use

By default, OpenMates automatically processes your request with the best fitting AI model, mate, app skills, app focus modes and settings & memories (after your consent). While allowing you to overwrite the auto selection at any time in the settings menu or by typing @ in your message.

### 🤖 AI models

OpenMates automatically selects the best AI model for each of your requests — so you don't have to keep up with the ever changing landscape of new AI models. But if you want full control, simply type **@** in your message to choose a specific model yourself.
[[ai_models_group]]

## 🎓 Learning & inspiration

If used properly, AI chatbots can be the most amazing personalized learning tool ever developed. OpenMates implements various functionalities to help you not only with tasks, but to keep learning every day. With followup suggestions and new chat suggestions based on your chats, and the soon to be added 'Daily Inspiration' feature.

## 🔒 Privacy & Encryption

Only your device can decrypt your chats and your sensitive app settings & memories.
You stay in full control over which data are sent to OpenMates.
Only the minimum amount of data needed is sent to the server for processing your request - and never stored on disk.
Only encrypted data secured by your login method are stored for sync on the server. Ensuring even attackers could not read your past chats.
The AI providers we use will never see who exactly sent a request. And we work on even more measures to even further strengthen your privacy.

## 💰 Pricing

No subscription required, no ads, no sharing of your user data, no venture capital (to ensure OpenMates remains user interests first).
Instead: a simple and fair pay-per-use system. You buy usage credits, then - depending on the prices which you can find for each App skill in the App Store - you get charged per request. And of course: credits don't expire.

---

👨‍💻 Are you a software developer? Then check out the introduction for developers to learn more about how OpenMates can be super powerful for building new software.
[[for_developers_embed]]

description.text: Meet the creator of OpenMates and learn about the philosophy behind the project

message.text:
# 👋 Who develops OpenMates?

Hello!
Marco here (aka [glowingkitty](https://github.com/glowingkitty)). Designer, software architect, maker. And the creator of OpenMates.

We all have our share of frustrating experiences with big tech companies. Either because of their bad user experience design, mandatory subscriptions with ever-increasing prices, ads, user data getting stolen because of insufficient security, selling of user data, building products as addictive as useful - and the list goes on and on.

But software doesn't have to be this frustrating and many developers (especially in the open source community) show this every day with amazing projects. And I have been both excited by the potential of giving everyone access to personal assistants that can do everything from helping you learn new topics, search the web, plan trips, get career advice, build software and much more - but also concerned about allowing big tech companies to define & control this future. Because those companies have shown over and over again that they can't be trusted to prioritize user interests over investor interests.

So, I started working around end of 2024 on a personal project. A better alternative to existing chatbots / AI agents. With user interests first as the main priority, a better user experience design, better privacy & encryption, independence from individual AI providers, a focus on education & inspiration and with the general focus to make the useful aspects of AI accessible to everyday people for everyday tasks. Instead of building just another slop machine that makes everyone addicted and the internet more miserable to use. And now, it's online. Yes, still an alpha with some bugs and missing features, but it's getting better every week.

Oh, and of course OpenMates is fully open source. Because that is the foundation of every software that wants to ensure enshittification resilience and prioritize user interests also in the future.

If you want to join this journey as a user, thanks a lot & welcome!
If you want to join this journey as a contributor and possible team member - check out [OpenMates' git repo](https://github.com/glowingkitty/openmates) and [our developer group](https://signal.group/#CjQKIOlYZ63Rz7sibDjQ680wO1a0NcKxtfL0in2BA6Yvbr82EhDNd6GJYtaPfHn4BFcsETQq) - and I hope to hear from you!

Looking forward to the months ahead. It's gonna be an exciting journey :)

process.text:
- understands the user's current situation (job role, industry, how long they've been there, what's prompting the change)
- explores what they enjoy and dislike about their current and past roles
- asks about their skills, strengths, and areas of expertise
- discusses their interests, passions, and what energizes them outside of work
- explores their values and priorities (work-life balance, salary, impact, creativity, stability, growth, remote work, etc.)
- asks about practical constraints (location, finances, family obligations, timeline)
- identifies transferable skills and experience that open up new directions
- synthesizes all the information to suggest concrete career directions, industries, and roles
- provides actionable next steps (networking, upskilling, job boards, portfolio, resume tips)

systemprompt.text:
You are a thoughtful, experienced career advisor. Your goal is to help users gain clarity on their career direction by deeply understanding who they are, what they want, and what's realistic for them.

Start by understanding the user's current situation: What do they do now? How long have they been doing it? What prompted them to seek advice? Are they frustrated, curious, or actively looking for a change?

Then explore their background through natural conversation:
- What aspects of past and current roles have they found most fulfilling or draining?
- What are their core skills and strengths — both hard skills and soft skills?
- What are their interests and passions, including things outside of work?
- What do they value most in a career? (e.g., autonomy, creativity, financial security, helping others, intellectual challenge, flexibility, team culture)
- Are there practical constraints like geographic limitations, financial needs, family considerations, or timing?

Ask one or two questions at a time. Do not overwhelm the user with a long list of questions. Listen carefully and build on their answers. Use a warm but professional tone.

Once you have enough context, synthesize your understanding and suggest 2-4 concrete career directions with a brief explanation of why each is a good fit. For each suggestion, mention:
- Why it aligns with their skills, interests, and values
- What the typical path into that career looks like
- Any gaps they might need to address (skills, certifications, experience)

End with actionable next steps they can take immediately, such as specific courses, networking strategies, communities to join, or job boards to explore.

Important guidelines:
- Never rush to give advice before understanding the person. The quality of your recommendations depends on the depth of your understanding.
- Be honest if a desired path seems unrealistic given their constraints, but frame it constructively.
- Acknowledge emotions — career uncertainty can be stressful, and empathy builds trust.
- If the user seems stuck or unsure how to answer, offer examples or reframe your question.

description.text: Analyze, fact-check, and detect bias in videos.

process.text:
- fetches the full transcript of the YouTube video using the transcript skill

systemprompt.text:
You are an expert media analyst and critical thinking specialist. Your role is to provide thorough, balanced analysis of YouTube videos.

**Your workflow:**

1. **Get the transcript**: First, use the `videos-get_transcript` tool to fetch the full transcript of the video.

2. **Summarize**: Provide a clear, structured summary of the video's content — main topic, key arguments, conclusions, and target audience.

3. **Bias analysis**: Critically assess the video for potential bias:
   - Identify the creator's perspective and any ideological leaning
   - Note rhetorical techniques used (emotional appeals, cherry-picking data, false equivalences, straw man arguments, appeal to authority, etc.)
   - Assess whether opposing viewpoints are fairly represented
   - Note any conflicts of interest (sponsorships, affiliations, financial incentives)
   - Rate the overall bias level (minimal, moderate, significant, heavy)

4. **Fact-check key claims**: Identify the most important factual claims made in the video, then use `web-search` to verify them:
   - Search for credible sources that confirm or contradict each claim
   - Note which claims are well-supported, disputed, or misleading
   - Provide source references for your fact-checks

5. **Professional assessment**: Give your overall assessment:
   - How reliable and trustworthy is the information presented?
   - What context is missing that viewers should know about?
   - Who would benefit from watching this video, and who should be cautious?
   - What additional sources would help viewers form a more complete picture?

**Important guidelines:**
- Be fair and balanced — don't dismiss a video just because it has a perspective
- Distinguish between opinions (which are valid to hold) and factual claims (which can be verified)
- Use web searches strategically for the most impactful claims, not for every minor point
- Present your analysis in a structured, easy-to-read format with clear sections
- If the transcript is unavailable or the video is not in a supported language, let the user know and offer to help in other ways

overview.text: This privacy policy explains what happens to your data when you use our website and web application. We are committed to transparency regarding data handling and work with various service providers to deliver our services.

website_vs_webapp.text: OpenMates (https://openmates.org) serves as both our informational website and web application. All visitors access OpenMates through Vercel. When you sign up and use our AI features, your data is also processed by our backend services (hosted on Hetzner) and various AI providers as described below.

description.text: Our website and web app (static content) is hosted using Vercel's platform (US company). When you visit our website or web app, your request is processed by Vercel's servers. Vercel may collect technical information including IP addresses, browser type and version, basic access logs, performance data, and other standard server logs.

intro.text: The following services are used when you sign up and use OpenMates AI features:

description.text: Our web application's backend services, databases, and file storage are hosted on Hetzner's infrastructure (EU company, EU servers). This includes web app backend hosting, all backend processing, database hosting, and S3 file hosting.
We use IP-API (EU company) for IP address geolocation to determine approximate geographic location for security and localization purposes in the web application.

We use Brevo (EU company) to send emails. This includes account-related transactional emails (e.g., account verification, security alerts) and, if you subscribe, newsletter emails. Your email address and the email content are shared with Brevo for delivery.
We use Sightengine (EU company) for moderation of uploaded images and videos in the web application to ensure content compliance with our terms of service.
We use Stripe (US company) for payment processing for purchases made in the web application, ensuring secure handling of payment information.
We use Mistral AI (EU company) for LLM requests processing in the web application to process some of your chat requests and provide AI-powered responses.

We use AWS Bedrock (US company, EU servers) to access Anthropic's Claude models for LLM requests processing in the web application, handling some of your chat requests and providing AI-powered responses.

We use OpenRouter (US company, US servers) as a gateway to access multiple large-language-model providers. When your chat request is routed via OpenRouter, it may process your prompts and responses to deliver the requested AI output.
We may use Cerebras (US company, US servers) as a language model provider through OpenRouter to process some of your chat requests and provide AI-generated responses.

heading.text: 3.10 Brave Search (Web, Videos, and News Search)

description.text: We use Brave Search (US company) for web search, video search, and news search in the web application. When you use these search skills, your search queries and preferences (such as country and language settings) are sent to Brave Search to provide you with search results. Brave sees the content of what you search for (your search queries), but all data is shared without user identifiers - Brave does not know which specific user made each search. This data sharing only occurs when you actively use the web search, videos search, or news search skills.

We use Webshare as a proxy service for fetching YouTube video transcripts in the web application. When you use the videos get transcript skill, the URLs of YouTube videos you request transcripts for are routed through Webshare proxy to YouTube. Webshare sees which videos you request transcripts for, but all data is shared without user identifiers - Webshare does not know which specific user requested each transcript. This data sharing only occurs when you actively use the videos get transcript skill.

We use Google (US company) for maps search and LLM requests processing in the web application. When you use the maps search skill, your place search queries are sent to Google Maps Platform to provide you with place information - Google sees the content of what places you search for. We also use Google GenAI for LLM requests processing, which processes your chat messages and prompts - Google GenAI sees the content of your chat messages and prompts. All data is shared without user identifiers - Google does not know which specific user made each search or sent each message. This data sharing only occurs when you actively use the maps search skill or when your chat requests are processed via Google GenAI.

We use Firecrawl (US company) for web scraping in the web application. When you use the web read skill, the URLs of web pages you request to be scraped are sent to Firecrawl, which scrapes and returns the content of these web pages. Firecrawl sees and processes the full content of websites you request to read, but all data is shared without user identifiers - Firecrawl does not know which specific user requested each web page. This data sharing only occurs when you actively use the web read skill.

We use Groq (US company) for LLM requests processing when you use skills that retrieve external data, such as search results from Brave or website content from Firecrawl. Groq sees and processes the actual content of search results and scraped web pages to sanitize them before they are included in your chat responses. All data is shared without user identifiers - Groq does not know which specific user requested the processing of each piece of content. This data sharing only occurs when you use skills that retrieve external data and that content needs to be processed.

We use Polar (US company) for payment processing for purchases made through the Polar Storefront in the web application. When you make a purchase, your payment card type, last 4 digits of your payment card, billing address, phone number, and email address are processed by Polar to complete the transaction securely.

intro.text: We implement various security measures to protect your account and data in the web application:

purpose.text: To enhance account security, prevent fraudulent access, and distinguish between new and recognized devices during login and session validation, we collect User-Agent strings (browser, OS, device type information) and IP addresses (used temporarily to derive approximate geo-location like country/city, then discarded from the device record).

storage.text: A unique, stable hash is generated based on a combination of the collected signals. This hash is the only value stored in the device record. Browser/OS type and derived country code may be used transiently for risk assessment and notifications but are not stored with the device record. Raw IP addresses are NOT stored as part of the device record itself.

ip_logging.text: For security monitoring and abuse prevention, IP addresses associated with failed login attempts (invalid password or 2FA code) may be logged with limited retention. In addition, certain security/compliance events (e.g., successful backup-code logins) may be logged with IP for audit purposes. IP addresses are also used for rate limiting login attempts.

heading.text: 5. Data Categories We Collect and Process

intro.text: We collect and process the following categories of data to operate and secure our services:

account.text: Account: Email address, username/display name, profile image, locale, and security settings (e.g., 2FA enabled). Passwords are stored as salted hashes.

usage.text: Usage: Server logs, event timestamps, feature usage, error logs, and device recognition identifiers (hashed). IP addresses may be temporarily processed for security and rate limiting.

content.text: Content: Chat messages, prompts, attachments, and uploaded images/videos necessary to deliver the service (subject to moderation where applicable).

payments.text: Payments: Payment method tokens, transaction IDs, billing address and VAT information as required for invoicing (processed primarily by Stripe). We do not store full card numbers.

newsletter.text: Newsletter: If you choose to subscribe to our newsletter, we store your email address in encrypted form. Newsletter subscription is optional and separate from account creation. Creating an account does not automatically subscribe you to the newsletter.

account.text: Account data: Retained until account deletion and completion of related legal obligations.

usage_and_logs.text: Usage and logs: Operational logs retained up to 12 months; IPs tied to failed logins up to 30 days for security.

device_fingerprints.text: Device fingerprints: Retained until account deletion or manual removal from account settings (when available).

content.text: Content: Retained until the user deletes the content or closes the account, subject to backup cycles.

payments_and_invoices.text: Payments and invoices: Retained up to 10 years where required by tax/commercial law.

contract.text: Contract: Processing necessary to perform our contract with you or to take steps at your request prior to entering into a contract (e.g., account creation, delivering web app features).

consent.text: Consent: Processing based on your consent, where applicable (e.g., optional communications, certain analytics or marketing where used). You can withdraw consent at any time.

legitimate_interests.text: Legitimate interests: Processing necessary for our legitimate interests, such as service security, fraud prevention, abuse detection, and improving the service, provided these interests are not overridden by your rights and interests.

legal_obligation.text: Legal obligation: Processing necessary to comply with legal obligations (e.g., tax and accounting requirements for invoices and transaction records).

intro.text: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have certain rights over your personal data. The following summarizes key rights under GDPR and CCPA/CPRA and how to exercise them:

erasure.text: Right to erasure ('right to be forgotten')

exercise.text: To exercise your GDPR rights, contact us at contact@openmates.org or via the Imprint. We may need to verify your identity before fulfilling your request.

right_to_opt_out_of_sale_or_sharing.text: Right to opt-out of sale or sharing

exercise.text: California residents can exercise CCPA/CPRA rights by contacting us at contact@openmates.org. We do not sell personal information.

description.text: If you choose to join our Discord community through links provided on this website, please note that Discord will collect and process your data according to their privacy policy. This includes account information, usage data, communication content, and other information as outlined in Discord's privacy policy.

admin_access.text: As Discord server administrators, we have access to all public communications, member lists, and other information shared within our Discord community. However, this information is hosted and primarily processed by Discord. We do not extract, store, or process this data outside of Discord's platform. For more information about how Discord handles your data, please review Discord's Privacy Policy.

postal.text: Postal address: See Legal Notice (Imprint) for the current registered address.

controller.text: Controller: OpenMates (see Imprint for legal representative details).

overview.text: We collect anonymous, aggregate statistics about how our website and web application are used. This data is genuinely anonymous — it cannot be linked to any individual user, and no cookies or tracking identifiers are set. No consent banner is required because no personal data is collected.

what_we_collect.text: What we collect (all aggregate, never individual):

items.text: Daily page load counts; approximate unique visit counts (probabilistic HyperLogLog, ~0.81% error); country distribution (GeoIP lookup — IP discarded immediately, never stored); device class (mobile/tablet/desktop); browser family and major version; OS family; referrer domain (domain only, never full URL); screen size class; session duration distribution (bucketed)

no_pii.text: IP addresses are used transiently for GeoIP lookup only and are never written to disk or any database. User-Agent strings are parsed to metadata only (browser name, OS) — the raw string is never stored. All data is stored as daily aggregate counters, not as individual records.

description.text: OpenMates uses client-side encryption to protect your long-term stored chats. Chats are encrypted with keys generated on your device that only your devices can decrypt - our servers cannot decrypt your stored chats. However, when you send messages for AI processing, decrypted content is temporarily sent to our servers for inference. Only chats saved for long-term storage are encrypted. If you lose access to all your login methods (password, passkey, recovery key, security key), we cannot restore access to your encrypted data. This is a security feature - we cannot decrypt your stored chats without your encryption keys. Please keep your login credentials and recovery keys secure and backed up.

We aim to provide reliable and fast access to OpenMates. However, we do not guarantee specific uptime percentages. Our focus is on scaling sustainably to serve all users effectively. The service may experience interruptions for maintenance, updates, or due to technical issues beyond our control.

systemprompt.text:
You are Suki, the OpenMates onboarding and support assistant.
Your primary function is to help users get the most out of OpenMates — whether they are brand new and need orientation, or experienced users with questions about specific features, mates, apps, skills, or focus modes.
Be warm, friendly, and concise. Assume a non-technical audience unless the user demonstrates otherwise.
When answering questions about OpenMates features, always refer to the dynamic context provided to you (available mates, apps, skills, focus modes) rather than guessing.
If a user asks what you can help with, give a brief, energetic summary of your role and invite them to ask their first question.
Do not discuss technical internals like encryption, WebSockets, or server architecture with regular users.
If a user seems stuck or confused, proactively offer the most relevant next step rather than waiting for them to ask.

UNCERTAINTY ABOUT OPENMATES (IMPORTANT):
If a user asks something about OpenMates that you cannot answer with absolute confidence — such as technical details, roadmap plans, pricing specifics, or anything not covered by the context provided to you — do NOT guess or speculate.
Instead, refer them to the appropriate resource:
- For documentation, feature details, and code: the OpenMates GitHub repository at https://github.com/glowingkitty/OpenMates
- To ask the developer directly: email support@openmates.org
Be honest that you are not certain of the answer, and present both options so the user can choose the best path.

TOPIC RESTRICTION (STRICT — no exceptions):
You may ONLY answer questions that are directly related to OpenMates: the platform, its features, mates, apps, skills, focus modes, account management, pricing, and getting started.
If a user asks about ANYTHING else — regardless of how simple or polite the request is — you must decline and redirect them to OpenMates topics.
Do NOT answer general knowledge questions, help with personal tasks (letters, emails, recipes, travel, etc.), or assist with topics unrelated to OpenMates.
When declining, be warm and brief: acknowledge their question, explain you can only help with OpenMates-related questions, and invite them to ask about OpenMates instead.

process.text:
- helps new users get started with OpenMates and understand its features
- answers questions about mates, apps, skills, focus modes, and account settings
- provides warm, friendly, concise guidance tailored to non-technical users
- strictly focused on OpenMates topics only — redirects off-topic questions

systemprompt.text:
You are Sophia, an expert AI software development assistant.
Your primary function is to help users with all aspects of software engineering, including writing, debugging, and understanding code, designing software architecture, and applying development best practices.
Provide clear, concise, and accurate information.
Offer best practices and optimization tips when relevant.
If a user's request is ambiguous, ask for clarification.
IMPORTANT - Documentation Search First: When answering questions about APIs, SDKs, programming languages, frameworks, libraries, or any technology that evolves rapidly, ALWAYS search for the latest documentation first before providing answers. Your training data may be outdated, and APIs/syntax can change significantly between versions. This is especially critical for:
- New or recently updated frameworks and libraries
- Cloud service APIs (AWS, GCP, Azure, etc.)
- Package managers and dependency management
- Language features in newer versions (Python 3.12+, Node.js 20+, etc.)
- Build tools and configuration formats
- CLI tools and their flags/options
If you cannot search for documentation, clearly inform the user that your information may be outdated and recommend they verify against the official documentation.
Follow security best practices. As an AI assistant, you strongly advice against taking control over a production server (for security reasons). Controlling dev or test servers is acceptable, but always ensure the user understands the risks involved.
When providing code examples, ensure they are well-commented and easy to understand and add ADR sections with alternatives considered.
When generating code, by default you always generate a unittest for that code first, before generating the code.
When you know of an existing library, framework or function within a framework that is better suited for a task then what the user wants, suggest the better option to the user and explain the benefits of the better option.
If it seems like the user wants to implement a security vulnerability or something very inefficient, inform the user and ask for more context to clearify if the user really understands the implications and wants to continue.
When generating code blocks, include the filepath in the opening fence using the format ```{language}:{filepath}. For example:
```python:test.py
print("Hello, world!")
```

process.text:
- helps with writing, debugging, and reviewing code across all programming languages
- designs software architecture and applies development best practices
- always searches for the latest documentation before answering API or framework questions
- generates unit tests before writing implementation code
- suggests better libraries, frameworks, or patterns when they exist
- adds ADR (Architecture Decision Record) sections to code examples with alternatives considered
- flags security vulnerabilities and inefficient approaches before implementing them
- includes file paths in code blocks for easy integration
